site.title: Wh Claw | Open Source AI
site.description: Free AI resources, open source tools, and educational content for the developer community. Sharing knowledge to make AI accessible to everyone.

subhero.heading: Making AI Accessible Through Open Source
subhero.paragraph1: I believe AI should be accessible to everyone, not just large tech companies with massive budgets. That's why I create and share free tools, tutorials, and resources for the developer community.
subhero.paragraph2: Everything here is open source and free to use. Whether you're a student learning ML, a startup building your first AI feature, or a researcher pushing boundaries—I hope these resources help you on your journey.

videoSection.heading: Learn AI at Your Own Pace
videoSection.paragraph1: I've created comprehensive tutorials and courses covering everything from ML fundamentals to advanced topics like LLMs and computer vision. All completely free, with code examples you can run yourself.
videoSection.paragraph2: The content is designed for hands-on learning—read, code, experiment, and build. No paywalls, no subscriptions, just knowledge shared openly with the community.

resources.heading: Open Source Tools & Libraries
resources.description: A collection of free, open-source AI tools, libraries, and resources I've built for the community. Use them, fork them, contribute to them— they're yours.

opensource.title: 100% Open Source
opensource.description: All code is MIT licensed. Use it commercially, modify it, contribute back—complete freedom to build what you need.

maintenance.title: Active Maintenance
maintenance.description: Projects are actively maintained with regular updates, bug fixes, and new features based on community feedback.

community.title: Community Driven
community.description: Built with input from the community. Feature requests, PRs, and discussions shape the direction of every project.

free.title: Free Forever
free.description: No freemium, no trials, no paywalls. Everything here is and always will be completely free for everyone.

post1.title: Building Your First Neural Network from Scratch
post1.excerpt: A step-by-step guide to understanding and implementing a neural network using only NumPy. No frameworks, just pure understanding.

post2.title: LLM Fine-tuning: A Practical Guide
post2.excerpt: Everything you need to know about fine-tuning large language models for your specific use case, with working code examples.

post3.title: MLOps for Solo Developers
post3.excerpt: Production-ready ML pipelines that don't require a team of DevOps engineers. Simple, effective approaches for small teams.

q1.question: Is everything really free?
q1.answer: Yes, absolutely. All tools, libraries, tutorials, and resources on this site are completely free and open source under the MIT license. You can use them for personal projects, commercial work, or anything else—no attribution required (though it's appreciated!).

q2.question: How can I contribute?
q2.answer: Contributions are welcome! You can submit bug reports, feature requests, or pull requests on GitHub. Even sharing the resources with others who might find them helpful is a valuable contribution to the community.

q3.question: What skill level are the tutorials for?
q3.answer: I try to make content accessible to a wide range of skill levels. Tutorials typically include both beginner-friendly explanations and advanced implementation details. If you're ever stuck, feel free to ask questions in the GitHub discussions.

q4.question: Can I use these tools in production?
q4.answer: Many of these tools are used in production by companies and individuals. However, as with any open source software, you should evaluate whether they meet your specific requirements. All projects include documentation about their current state and any known limitations.

q5.question: How do you sustain this work?
q5.answer: I maintain these projects in my spare time as a way to give back to the community that taught me. If you find value in my work and want to support it, the best way is to contribute code, report bugs, or simply share the resources with others.

section1.heading: A Developer Giving Back
section1.paragraph1: I'm Alex, a software engineer who's been fortunate to work with AI at some amazing companies. Along the way, I've learned from countless open source projects, free tutorials, and generous community members who shared their knowledge.
section1.paragraph2: This site is my way of paying it forward. I create tools I wish existed, write tutorials I wish I'd had, and share everything openly so others can learn and build faster than I did.
section1.paragraph3: When I'm not coding or writing, you'll find me hiking, reading, or mentoring aspiring developers. I believe technology is at its best when it's shared freely and built collaboratively.

section2.heading: Knowledge Wants to Be Free
section2.quote: The best way to learn is to teach. The best way to grow is to share. Open source isn't just about code—it's about building a better future together.

contact.heading: Let's Connect
contact.description: Have a question, suggestion, or just want to chat about AI? I'd love to hear from you. Reach out anytime.

form.messagePlaceholder: Share your thoughts, questions, or ideas...
form.success: Thanks for reaching out! I'll get back to you as soon as I can.

footer.description: Open source AI tools, free tutorials, and educational resources for the developer community. Building a more accessible future for AI.

newsletter.heading: Stay Updated
newsletter.description: Get notified about new tools, tutorials, and blog posts. No spam, just valuable content.
